title: convert max_web_research_loops to int to make sure comparison works
body:
This PR fixes a bug introduced by my previous PR and the getting the max_web_research_loops from the environment variable. I'm not sure why it is a string value when doing the `if state.research_loop_count <= int(configurable.max_web_research_loops):` compare, I fixed it with just another `int()` around it.

Sorry for that!

step_plan.summary: Fixes loop-gating logic by ensuring `max_web_research_loops` is always an integer when loaded from environment variables or RunnableConfig overrides, preventing invalid `int` vs `str` comparisons during research routing.

steps.operation: Coerce max_web_research_loops override values to int (env + RunnableConfig) before constructing Configuration
steps.reason: `os.environ.get()` and `RunnableConfig['configurable']` values can be strings; dataclasses don’t coerce types, so `Configuration(**values)` can end up with `max_web_research_loops` as `str`, breaking numeric comparisons elsewhere. Normalizing here makes the Configuration object the single source of truth and fixes all downstream uses.
steps.side_effects: If the provided value is non-numeric (e.g., 'three'), `int(...)` will raise `ValueError` unless handled; this may surface configuration errors earlier. Any callers relying on string values (unlikely/incorrect) will now receive an int.

steps.operation: Remove/avoid ad-hoc casting at the comparison site and rely on Configuration typing for loop gating
steps.reason: Once `Configuration.from_runnable_config` guarantees `max_web_research_loops` is an int, `route_research` should compare `state.research_loop_count` directly to `configurable.max_web_research_loops` to prevent scattered type patches and keep logic clean.
steps.side_effects: If some code path bypasses `Configuration.from_runnable_config` and supplies an untyped `configurable` object, the comparison could still fail; consider ensuring all graph entrypoints build config via `from_runnable_config`. Update/adjust any unit/integration tests that previously expected an extra `int()` cast here.
